message:
beads: add 4 close-only entries from concurrent close-mode sweep

Tracker bookkeeping only. No code changes.

The concurrent close-mode dispatch pass landed four new entries in
.beads/issues.jsonl, all marked closed as a deliberate "record and
close" pattern — this is the bd/br convention for retroactively
filing a bead that was already fixed before it could be tracked,
so the close_reason stays on-record even though no open/in_progress
state ever existed.

Added bead IDs and their landing SHAs (per the close_reason field
on each entry):

- ft-e018k  closed — landed earlier
- ft-hsmjb  closed — landed earlier
- ft-scoz7  closed — landed earlier
- ft-w6z9m  closed — landed earlier

Specific landing SHAs live in each bead's close_reason. Rolling
them up into a single index commit here keeps the tracker
synchronised with the main branch without creating four
single-line commits.

files.patch:

 {"id":"ft-d6oxc","title":"add golden session restore JSON contract coverage","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:43:56.170915Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:27.689268Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:10:27.688265Z","close_reason":"Verified in d6237b5d; HEAD now contains session_restore_json_contract_golden","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-dijpe","title":"connector_inbound_bridge: dedup uses attacker-controlled signal.timestamp_ms as 'now', lets replay bypass the window","description":"# Defect

`crates/frankenterm-core/src/connector_inbound_bridge.rs:495` in `route_signal`:

```rust
if let Some(ref cid) = signal.correlation_id {
    let now_ms = signal.timestamp_ms;
    if !self.deduplicator.check_and_record(cid, now_ms) {
        ...
    }
}
```

The bridge uses `signal.timestamp_ms` — explicitly documented at line 132 as 'Timestamp of the signal AT THE SOURCE' — as the 'now_ms' argument that drives dedup eviction. Every connector (github, slack, webhook, etc.) sends this field and the bridge trusts it as its clock.

## Concrete replay bypass

`SignalDeduplicator::check_and_record` calls `evict_expired(now_ms)` which pops entries where `now_ms - inserted > ttl_ms`. An attacker-controlled upstream can:

1. Send signal X with `correlation_id='spam'`, `timestamp_ms=0` → inserted_at=0 in cache.
2. Send signal X again with `correlation_id='spam'`, `timestamp_ms=ttl_ms+1` → evict_expired sees `now=ttl+1`, front-entry at inserted=0, `ttl+1 > ttl` → pops 'spam' from cache. Then check finds cache empty → inserts a new 'spam' entry → returns TRUE (treated as new). Dedup bypassed.

Repeat indefinitely by claiming monotonically-increasing timestamps. Every signal is seen as 'new' and routed to the pattern engine + EventBus, duplicating every downstream side effect (workflow triggers, storage writes, webhook cascades).

## Scope

Only bites the inbound bridge, whose callers ARE the external connectors (by design — this is the agent-owned ingest surface for webhook/API signals). A buggy connector that sends stale `timestamp_ms` values can unknowingly bypass dedup too; it doesn't require malice.

Outbound bridge doesn't have this shape — it uses its own `now_ms` from `SystemTime::now()` for all internal state.

## Fix

Drive dedup bookkeeping from the LOCAL system clock, not the signal's claimed timestamp. The signal.timestamp_ms field stays useful as analytics/telemetry data (end-to-end source→sink latency, etc.) but must NOT determine when dedup entries expire.

Change `route_signal` to:

```rust
let now_ms = SystemTime::now()
    .duration_since(UNIX_EPOCH)
    .unwrap_or_default()
    .as_millis() as u64;
if !self.deduplicator.check_and_record(cid, now_ms) { ... }
```

## Regression test

- `dedup_rejects_replay_with_future_timestamp`: route X with cid='r' and timestamp=0, then route X with cid='r' and timestamp=ttl+1. Assert the second is still flagged `deduplicated=true` under a fresh bridge with a short ttl. Before the fix, the second call returns `deduplicated=false` because evict_expired sees now=ttl+1 and pops 'r'.

## Severity

MEDIUM — requires an external caller to either claim stale/future timestamps or replay signals. Doesn't crash. But every downstream connector action (Slack post, JIRA ticket, GitHub PR comment) can be re-fired without bound by a single misbehaving connector.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane0","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:45:15.242201Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:21.688022Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:50:21.687664Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 0716e112. Replaced signal.timestamp_ms → SystemTime::now() in route_signal's dedup clock argument so an upstream claiming future timestamps can no longer evict live dedup entries and bypass the window. Regression test bridge_dedup_ignores_attacker_controlled_signal_timestamp (timestamp_ms=u64::MAX/2 on replay → still flagged deduplicated=true) covers the attacker-replay shape. Verified: 44 connector_inbound_bridge:: tests passed, 0 failed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-dqsev","title":"[bug] delete_session runs 3 DELETEs without a transaction — partial failure yields orphaned checkpoints","description":"# Bug

\`session_restore::delete_session\` at \`crates/frankenterm-core/src/session_restore.rs:1003\`:

\`\`\`rust
pub fn delete_session(db_path: &str, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool, RestoreError> {
    let conn = open_conn(db_path)?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id IN ...", [session_id])?;
    conn.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    let deleted = conn.execute("DELETE FROM mux_sessions WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    Ok(deleted > 0)
}
\`\`\`

Three independent DELETEs, each in its own implicit rusqlite
transaction. If step 1 succeeds but step 2 fails (lock contention,
disk full, IO error, cancellation), we leave the database in a
tornn-inconsistent state:

  - Option A: pane_states DELETED, checkpoints still present,
    session still present → \`load_checkpoint_by_id\` returns
    Some(checkpoint) but with empty \`pane_states\` → restore
    produces an empty-pane session that looks clean but carries no
    terminal state.

  - Option B: pane_states + checkpoints DELETED, session row
    still present → \`list_sessions\` shows the session, but
    \`load_latest_checkpoint\` returns None. Next \`delete_session\`
    call's third DELETE succeeds → session is eventually cleaned
    up, but the window is observable.

The other deletion-style paths in session_restore.rs DO wrap their
mutations in \`conn.transaction()\` (see lines 626, 673). delete_session
is the anomaly.

# Fix

Wrap the three DELETEs in a single \`conn.transaction()\` that
commits on success or rolls back on any intermediate error.

\`\`\`rust
pub fn delete_session(db_path: &str, session_id: &str) -> Result<bool, RestoreError> {
    let mut conn = open_conn(db_path)?;
    let tx = conn.transaction()?;
    tx.execute("DELETE FROM mux_pane_state WHERE checkpoint_id IN ...", [session_id])?;
    tx.execute("DELETE FROM session_checkpoints WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    let deleted = tx.execute("DELETE FROM mux_sessions WHERE session_id = ?1", [session_id])?;
    tx.commit()?;
    Ok(deleted > 0)
}
\`\`\`

# Test

Regression test: inject mid-operation failure (e.g., by DELETING a
session that references a checkpoint whose pane_state rows have a
trigger or hook that fails). Without a transaction, the partial
state is observable; with the fix, the session row remains.

For a simpler test, assert that after a successful delete_session
there are zero rows in all three tables for the target session_id,
AND that commit/rollback atomicity holds across a wrapping PRAGMA.

# Scope

Single function, ~6 LOC change. No schema migration. Preserves the
return value contract (true if session row was deleted).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:51:06.907980Z","created_by":"pane3-delete-tx","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:29.944931Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:53:29.944608Z","close_reason":"Landed in 1a090629. Wrapped delete_session's three DELETE statements in a single conn.transaction() with commit on success. Pattern lifted from existing transactional helpers at session_restore.rs:626 + :673. Full cargo test blocked on ft-golgl pre-existing breakage — change is mechanical and preserves the return-value contract.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["atomicity","session","storage"]}
+{"id":"ft-e018k","title":"event_identity_key nested Object insertion order leaks into hash (recursive fix for 765743d5)","description":"765743d5 sorted top-level keys of extracted object, but nested Object/Array values went through serde_json::to_string which preserves IndexMap insertion order. Two logically-equivalent events with nested {'context': {'a':1,'b':2}} vs {'context': {'b':2,'a':1}} hashed differently → dedup-to-two. Fix landed in 50ff1eb0: canonicalize_json_value recursively sorts all nested Object keys before serialization (arrays stay ordered per JSON spec, but their contained Objects get canonicalized). Regression tests event_identity_key_ignores_nested_extracted_object_insertion_order + event_identity_key_canonicalizes_objects_inside_arrays.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:08:33.936724Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:25.227773Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:25.227512Z","close_reason":"Landed in 50ff1eb0 (fix(events): recursively canonicalize nested Object keys in normalized_extracted)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-e0ssu","title":"policy rate limiter global rejection creates empty per-pane buckets","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T11:39:37.455407Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:42:19.194643Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:42:19.193768Z","close_reason":"Prevented global-limit rejections from allocating empty per-pane buckets, pruned empty stale pane buckets during check, and added a regression that repeated global rejections do not leak pane entries.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","title":"[asupersync][vendored] Mux client/pool/runtime migration completion","description":"Drive completion of DirectMuxClient/MuxPool/runtime-adjacent migrations and align semantics with core doctrine.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Remaining runtime-incompatible primitives in mux client/pool paths are migrated or isolated with explicit adapters.\n2. Cancellation and backpressure semantics match doctrine and are documented.\n3. Unit tests cover mux path behavior under cancellation/retry/load.\n4. Integration tests validate core<->mux interactions after migration.\n5. E2E scripts verify mux reliability and structured diagnostics under stress.","notes":"This task should absorb and harmonize ongoing slices so we converge on one coherent mux runtime model. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:26:19.410169Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T16:59:26.700552Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mux","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-2h5wv","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","depends_on_id":"ft-p48pw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2449,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"SilverBluff starting a bounded 2026-03-22 support slice under ft-e34d9.10.5.2. Scope is test-only: use the existing clean integration surface in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs to add any missing timeout-cascade and recovery-path assertions without touching the active mux_client.rs implementation work. Goal is to strengthen the mux migration verification lane while staying off adjacent in-flight source files.","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:17:19Z"},{"id":2450,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"Progress 2026-03-22 (SilverBluff): landed a bounded test-only support slice in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Added two missing assertions on the clean integration surface without touching active mux_client.rs work: (1) pool_timeout_cascade_then_recovery_restores_capacity, which proves a timed-out nested acquire under capacity pressure does not leak permits or issue spurious mux reads and that the next healthy retry succeeds after release; (2) simulated_network_recovery_after_transient_fault_delivers_new_data, which proves a transient hostile-network fault can be followed by a healthy retry that delivers new payload bytes on the same underlying mock transport. Local hygiene checks are green: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs ✅ and git diff --check on that file ✅. Targeted remote validation is in flight via rch on vmi1153651 using: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-silverbluff-ft-e34d9-10-5-2 CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored recovery -- --test-threads=1. Current remote state: real compilation is progressing (not in immediate source-error or stale state), but rch emitted the existing dependency-topology fail-open warning for sibling path deps ([RCH-E327], primary-root-only sync).,workdir:","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:26:55Z"},{"id":2451,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SilverBluff","text":"Follow-up 2026-03-22 (SilverBluff): I cancelled rch build 29759189920253462 after ~9 minutes because it was still in a healthy first-compile dependency walk on vmi1153651 and I was no longer waiting on the session. Important distinction: no immediate source-level compile failure surfaced before cancellation; the run had progressed through substantial dependency compilation after the existing [RCH-E327] dependency-topology fail-open warning. This slice should be treated as code-complete but still awaiting a resumed targeted rch validation pass if fresh remote evidence is needed.","created_at":"2026-03-22T03:28:18Z"},{"id":2544,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"JadeBeaver","text":"JadeBeaver bounded support slice 2026-03-27 under ft-e34d9.10.5.2: explicit-Cx cancellation was being ignored/misclassified at the runtime_compat semaphore + generic Pool boundary, which let vendored mux-pool explicit-Cx paths surface timeout semantics or proceed instead of failing as cancellation. Landed fix in runtime_compat.rs, pool.rs, vendored/mux_pool.rs, and a small downstream guard in wezterm.rs so PoolError::Cancelled does not trip the vendored circuit breaker. Added focused regressions for pre-cancelled pool/mux-pool operations and mid-wait permit cancellation, plus proptest coverage for the new PoolError variant. Local rustfmt --check and git diff --check are green. Remote evidence is in progress via valid offloaded runs 58601 and 77601; a separate run 66978 failed the remote dependency preflight with RCH-E324 and fell open local, so it was rejected and not used as validation evidence.","created_at":"2026-03-27T18:24:36Z"},{"id":2612,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Support slice ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2 completed on the clean integration harness only: crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Added pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity, which proves a cancelled nested acquire while the only pool permit is held surfaces cancellation, preserves zero available permits, does not increment total_acquired, does not issue an extra mock mux request, and still allows the next healthy release/retry path to restore balance. Remote validation passed via rch on worker vmi1153651 with env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1 => 1 passed / 0 failed. Agent Mail is intermittently returning transient DB errors again, so this parent comment is the durable coordination record for the completion.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:32:05Z"},{"id":3574,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","author":"jemanuel","text":"cod1 support slice 2026-04-19: landed main commit 6a6f4ec5 on the clean integration harness only.  {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","title":"[ft-e34d9.10.5.2][support] Strengthen cancellation-under-contention invariants in mux migration completion harness","description":"Bounded test-only support slice under ft-e34d9.10.5.2. Scope is limited to crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs. Strengthen the currently shallow cancellation coverage by adding a contention-focused invariant: a cancelled acquire while the only pool permit is held must fail cleanly without leaking capacity or causing spurious downstream mux activity, and the subsequent healthy release/retry path must restore a balanced pool. Validate with rch-only targeted cargo evidence.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SunnyRaven","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:39.606026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-30T00:31:08.441229Z","closed_at":"2026-03-30T00:31:08.441066Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","cancellation","integration-tests","test-fix","vendored"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:39.606026Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2605,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Started bounded support slice on crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs only. Plan: strengthen cancellation-under-contention coverage so a cancelled nested acquire while the only pool permit is held fails cleanly without leaking capacity or causing spurious mux activity, then prove the subsequent healthy release/retry path restores balance. File reservation is active via Agent Mail #6191; validation will be rch-only targeted cargo.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:11:57Z"},{"id":2607,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Patch landed in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs only. Added a new cancellation-under-contention regression that mirrors the existing timeout-cascade recovery shape but uses user cancellation and stronger invariants: blocked acquire must surface cancellation, preserve zero available permits while the primary checkout is held, avoid incrementing total_acquired, and avoid issuing extra mock mux requests before a healthy release/retry restores balance. Local rustfmt and git diff --check are green. Targeted remote validation is in flight via rch on worker vmi1153651: env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:17:11Z"},{"id":2611,"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.2.2","author":"SunnyRaven","text":"Remote validation completed cleanly on worker vmi1153651. Command: env TMPDIR=/tmp CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target/rch-ft-e34d9-10-5-2-2 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --test mux_migration_completion --features asupersync-runtime,vendored pool_cancelled_cx_under_contention_then_recovery_restores_capacity -- --test-threads=1. Result: 1 passed / 0 failed. This confirms the new regression in crates/frankenterm-core/tests/mux_migration_completion.rs correctly proves a cancelled nested acquire under contention does not leak permits, does not count as a successful checkout, does not issue an extra mux request, and still allows the following healthy release/retry path to restore a balanced pool.","created_at":"2026-03-30T00:30:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-h8u5y","title":"[gap] ft-xbnl0 completion evidence cargo-check pass not matched by current compile blockers","description":"docs/ft-xbnl0-5-7-completion-evidence.md:18-27 claims `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-jemanuel-target cargo check -p frankenterm` passed remotely; rerunning on April 21, 2026 with `rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-codex-reality-target cargo check -p frankenterm` now fails with compile blockers at crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/sse.rs:231, crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:3433-3437, and crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:1921-2019.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"docs","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:24.027291Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T20:16:04.289965Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T20:16:04.289515Z","close_reason":"Verified workspace cargo check still FAILS on 2026-04-21, but blockers have rotated since the bead was filed:

Original bead citations (from code at time of filing):
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/web/sse.rs:231
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs:3433-3437
- crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_client.rs:1921-2019

Reinspection today: those three specific line ranges now contain clean code (closing brace/impl header at sse.rs:231, normal debug! logging at runtime.rs:3433, normal loop body at mux_client.rs:1921). The original blockers appear resolved by the intervening 9e1bd63b cfg-strip + c1fb747e mux_client delimiter fix cascade.

Current blockers (via rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target cargo check --workspace --all-targets):

 {"id":"ft-htf0i","title":"[HIGH] GUI resize increment saturation can overflow backend min-size arithmetic","description":"Commit 193a9ebb changes `ResizeIncrementCalculator` to saturate `base_width`/`base_height` at the u16 ceiling (`crates/frankenterm-gui/src/resize_increment_calculator.rs:15-35`). Multiple window backends then immediately compute `base + increment` in u16 without guarding overflow (`frankenterm/window/src/os/macos/window.rs:1410-1412`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/x11/window.rs:1892-1893`, `frankenterm/window/src/os/wayland/window.rs:915-916`). With large padding/border/tab-bar geometry, the new saturation can produce `base_width == u16::MAX`, so `base_width + x` overflows before min-size clamping. This turns an intended overflow hardening change into a runtime panic/wraparound path on oversized layouts. Expected fix: clamp stored base dimensions to `u16::MAX - increment` (or widen backend arithmetic) and add a regression test that asserts `base + increment` stays representable.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T05:57:00.591671Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T06:45:12.138080Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T06:45:12.137486Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-hyrav","title":"events::NotificationCooldown::check: insertion_order not refreshed on cooldown-expired send, LRU evicts actively-used keys","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/events.rs:1015-1064 NotificationCooldown::check has a subtle LRU-staleness bug: when a key's cooldown has EXPIRED and we emit Send (line 1036-1043), we update entry.last_notified to now and reset entry.suppressed_since_notify, but we do NOT move the key to the back of self.insertion_order. The key stays at its ORIGINAL insertion position. Next time the entries map hits max_capacity, the eviction path at line 1047-1050 pops insertion_order.pop_front() — which picks a key by ORIGINAL INSERTION ORDER, not by last-use. A busy key that's been 'refreshed' (send + reset) many times can be evicted while a truly-dormant key with a later insertion stays. Effect: the cooldown state is silently dropped for the recently-used key; subsequent check() sees no entry and treats it as a first occurrence, flooding out a notification that should have been suppressed or at least carried a 'suppressed_since_last' count. Same pattern already fixed in sibling EventDeduplicator at events.rs:867-870 (refresh insertion_order on window-expired reset). Fix: in the cooldown-expired Send branch, remove the key from its current insertion_order position and push_back — matching the EventDeduplicator pattern. Regression test: 3-capacity cooldown, insert A/B/C, advance time past cooldown + re-check A (causing expired Send + refresh), insert D (triggering eviction). Pre-fix A is evicted; post-fix B is evicted (truly oldest-by-use).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:33:24.607054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:34:50.222010Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:34:50.221646Z","close_reason":"Landed in 36c6f7d3 (fix(events): refresh NotificationCooldown insertion_order on cooldown-expired send). Mirrors EventDeduplicator's window-expired refresh pattern at events.rs:867-870. Regression test cooldown_expired_send_refreshes_lru_position_ft_hyrav pins the post-fix eviction order. Verification blocked by pre-existing frankenterm-core build blockers (ft-kqidb/uigpj/s2u51/zv3u9).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-ibtjf","title":"[gh-52] build.rs must populate FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV for path-dep workspace layout","description":"## Problem (GitHub #52)

After the fail-closed guard from #44, `ft doctor` unconditionally reports `[ERR] WezTerm vendored - vendored commit not recorded` on every clean-machine `cargo install --git ...` build. Root cause:

- `crates/frankenterm-core/build.rs` only extracts vendored commit from `Cargo.lock` entries whose `source = "git+...#<hash>"` fragment carries a commit.
- The default workspace layout declares `mux` and `wezterm-term` as **path dependencies** (`frankenterm/mux`, `frankenterm/term`), so Cargo does NOT emit a `source` field for them.
- `select_vendored_metadata` returns `(None, None, None)`, no `cargo:rustc-env=FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV=...` is emitted, `option_env!("FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_REV")` resolves to `None`, and `vendored.rs` reports `Incompatible` at runtime.

Runtime behavior is safe (ft correctly falls back to CLI backend) but the permanent `[ERR]` on default builds is a UX regression that hides real incompatibilities.

## Fix direction

In `crates/frankenterm-core/build.rs`, add a path-dep fallback branch to `select_vendored_metadata`:

1. If `mux.source.is_none()` (path dep), resolve the vendored subdir (`frankenterm/mux` relative to workspace root) and run `git -C <that-dir> rev-parse HEAD` to populate the commit.
2. Handle the case where the workspace is not a git checkout (e.g. cargo install tarball) — in that case, either fall back to a deterministic build-time hash (e.g. `sha256` of the mux crate source) or treat as "local-path-build" sentinel so vendored.rs can accept it without [ERR].
3. Add `cargo:rerun-if-changed=` entries for `frankenterm/mux/Cargo.toml` and `frankenterm/term/Cargo.toml` so the metadata refreshes on vendored bumps.
4. Emit `FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_VERSION` + `FT_WEZTERM_VENDORED_SOURCE` in the path-dep branch too.

## Acceptance criteria

1. `cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git --bin ft frankenterm && ft doctor` no longer produces `[ERR] WezTerm vendored - vendored commit not recorded`.
2. `ft doctor` reports a real `[OK]` or a meaningful vendored mismatch when the path-dep tree has drifted.
3. Runtime vendored compatibility guard (`vendored.rs:155-166`) continues to fail-closed for truly-missing metadata — path-dep fallback must not weaken it.
4. Build script handles non-git workspaces (cargo-install tarball extraction) without panicking.
5. Unit test or build-script harness covers: git-path-dep / non-git-path-dep / git+hash / missing-both.

## Reporter

GitHub user JordanChoo — https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm/issues/52","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T02:27:51.238506Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T02:50:52.805074Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T02:50:52.804717Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 3174399d (build.rs path-dep fallback + non-git sentinel + 8-test harness)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-s9oci","title":"mux/domain follow-ups: blocking spawn_command in async, silent write! errors","description":"Deferred during /multi-pass-bug-hunting audit shipped as dcaf60ca. Three pass-1/2 findings were clean in domain.rs, but two design-level issues remain as follow-up work:

1. Blocking spawn inside async fn. LocalDomain::spawn_pane (domain.rs:667) calls pair.slave.spawn_command(cmd) which on Unix is a synchronous fork+exec. If the child binary or an interposed fork hook hangs, the async task's executor thread is blocked indefinitely. Fix direction: route the spawn through tokio::task::spawn_blocking (or the asupersync equivalent) and await the join handle. Scope: also audit pair.master.take_writer() on the same path.

2. Silently swallowed write! errors. LocalDomain::spawn_pane on the child-spawn-failure branch (domain.rs:693) renders the error into the new pane via write!(writer, "{err:#}").ok(). If the write itself fails the user sees a blank pane with no indication of the upstream spawn failure. Fix direction: log the nested error via tracing::warn! with both the original spawn err and the write err so the failure is at least visible in logs.

Auditor: pane 6 (2026-04-20). Audit context: 91% context budget, committed primary fix + this bead for the residual scope before /clear.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-21T00:59:03.628753Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:59:35.409355Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:59:35.409051Z","close_reason":"Fixed in 5c8c417c. Item 1: spawn_command now runs via promise::spawn::spawn_into_new_thread so fork+exec no longer blocks the async executor thread. Item 2: silent write! errors were already fixed earlier in f6019f0f (replaced .ok() with and_then(flush)+tracing::warn! on both err paths).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["async-safety","domain","follow-up","mux"]}
+{"id":"ft-scoz7","title":"disk_pressure PressureThresholds::normalized() NaN slip-through (companion to ft-761tz threshold side)","description":"ft-761tz guarded usage_fraction.is_nan() in classify_tier but PressureThresholds::normalized() still used f64::clamp which preserves NaN. Misconfigured threshold (NaN yellow/red/black from permissive JSON/TOML parsers or buggy caller) produced Green for any usage including 1.0 — reinstating the exact ft-761tz bug on the config side. Fix landed in b0cce4da: snap NaN fields to Default before clamp. Regression test thresholds_with_nan_fields_fall_back_to_defaults_instead_of_green pinned 3 shapes (single-NaN-black, single-NaN-red, all-NaN).","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:08:32.141295Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:23.215354Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:23.215167Z","close_reason":"Landed in b0cce4da (fix(disk_pressure): NaN thresholds fall back to Default in PressureThresholds::normalized)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-sl1xk","title":"shell_escape must not turn empty restore cwd into bare cd","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"pane8","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:31:45.320983Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T09:39:05.107651Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T09:39:05.107317Z","close_reason":"already landed in 3b525792","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-swa8k","title":"[SEV] workflows/runner.rs orphan-cx fallbacks break ambient-cx inheritance in 3 sites","description":"crates/frankenterm-core/src/workflows/runner.rs lines 729, 1498, 2021 all use the stale fallback pattern:

  let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();
  let injector_cx = cx.unwrap_or(&request_cx);

When the caller passes cx=None but an ambient Cx is present (Cx::current() is Some), this falls back to a brand-new for_request() cx instead of inheriting the ambient one. That is the exact bug commit 02a599a9 (refactor(workflows): inherit ambient cx in pane injection wrappers) fixed for the four WorkflowContext::send_* entry points by switching to:

  let cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);

Concrete consequences:
- Line 729 (run_workflow_inner replay_capture setup): set_decision_capture runs under an orphan cx, so a parent cancellation cannot propagate through the capture adapter.
- Line 1498 (run_workflow_inner step loop send_text branch): policy-gated send_text for legacy non-cx callers injects under an orphan cx; parent cancellation does not abort the injection or its audit write.
- Line 2021 (pub async fn run event loop child spawn): runtime_compat::task::spawn_with_cx is invoked with a fresh cx, orphaning the spawned workflow from the runtime's ambient cx — cancelling the ambient cx does NOT cancel the child workflow.

Real non-test callers hit these paths: mcp_tools.rs:2145 calls runner.run_workflow(...) from an MCP handler (MCP handlers run under an ambient request cx), so MCP-initiated workflows run to completion even if the MCP request is cancelled. The run() event loop at line 1955 is the non-cx sibling of run_with_cx (line 2154) and can be invoked from inside a with_cx block — children spawned there escape the ambient scope.

Fix: replace each 'let request_cx = crate::cx::for_request();' with the ambient-inheriting pattern 'let request_cx = crate::cx::Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(crate::cx::for_request);'. No behaviour change when no ambient cx is present; closes the ambient-inheritance gap for every caller that already has one.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-20T22:07:15.266712Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-20T22:12:37.792231Z","closed_at":"2026-04-20T22:12:37.792041Z","close_reason":"Completed: replaced 3 stale for_request() fallbacks with Cx::current().unwrap_or_else(for_request) in runner.rs (replay_capture setup, step-loop send_text, run() event-loop child spawn). cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib passes clean. Commit 09014aae.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-szuzd","title":"mcp_tools: schema-declared numeric maxima on wa.cass_* and wa.wait_for/wa.send are not enforced by handlers","description":"Several wa.* MCP tools declare numeric bounds in their input_schema but only enforce the LOWER bound at runtime. The UPPER bound is silently ignored because serde_json doesn't honour JSON-Schema's maximum (that's exactly why ft-tzwuw added the explicit timeout_secs == 0 check). Same shape applies to the maximum side:

- crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs:526 wa.cass_search declares 'timeout_secs: {minimum: 1, maximum: 600}' but only checks == 0 at :572. A client sending timeout_secs: 3600 is accepted; cass_client_with_timeout(3600) builds a 1-hour timeout; cass can block the mcp server for up to an hour.
- Same file wa.cass_search declares 'limit: {minimum: 0, maximum: 1000}' with no runtime bound at all — a client sending limit: u64::MAX stages that into CassSearchOptions and the downstream cass call.
- wa.cass_view (:657), wa.cass_expand (:763), wa.wait_for (:1202-area), wa.send (:1926-area) repeat the same shape for timeout_secs.

wa.events at :1725-1749 shows the gold-standard pattern (LIMIT_MIN/LIMIT_MAX constants + matching error message). Fix: replicate that pattern for every tool whose schema declares a minimum/maximum that the handler doesn't already enforce. Scope this bead to wa.cass_search first (both bounds); sibling tools follow-on with pointers to this bead.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:57:22.700960Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T18:00:36.248624Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T18:00:36.248425Z","close_reason":"Landed in 4dc2d752 — 132 lines of mcp_tools.rs changes bundled with another agent's session_restore/snapshot_engine ft-rfpk6 fix (concurrent-agent omnibus commit). The bundled changes are exactly the wa.cass_search timeout_secs + limit upper-bound guards + three regression tests (ft_tzwuw_ca_search_rejects_zero_timeout_secs updated to range form, ft_szuzd_ca_search_rejects_above_max_timeout_secs, ft_szuzd_ca_search_rejects_above_max_limit). Parallel gaps in wa.cass_view/wa.cass_expand/wa.wait_for/wa.send remain — follow-on sweeps can mirror this same pattern.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

+{"id":"ft-w6z9m","title":"ft-up6u2 read_capped_file metadata-Err silent fallthrough to unbounded read_to_string","description":"ft-up6u2 (3e2ae80c) used stat-first + post-read length check for DoS defense on mission+tx JSON files. If metadata() returns Err (network FS stat blocked, permission error), the guard silently falls through to read_to_string which allocates the full file before the post-read check catches it — still OOMs on huge files from pseudo-FS or TOCTOU swap. Fix landed in 36d0d47e: replaced read_to_string with streaming File::open + Read::take(max_bytes+1).read_to_end. The I/O layer itself caps memory now; stat-lie attack from procfs-style or networked FS cannot OOM. Preserves UTF-8 validation and error envelope shape.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T21:08:34.867319Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:26.219856Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T21:09:26.219584Z","close_reason":"Landed in 36d0d47e (fix(mcp_missions): switch read_capped_file to streaming bounded read)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-wajba","title":"tmux cmd_queue drop-head-in-flight corrupts Guarded response routing","description":"frankenterm/mux/src/tmux.rs:73-91 (push_command_capped) vs :165-186 (Guarded response handler).

push_command_capped(queue, cmd) does queue.drain(..excess) when queue exceeds CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH=50_000. The drain is from the FRONT. But when TmuxDomainState.state == WaitingForResponse, the front of cmd_queue is the in-flight command — send_next_command only peeks front() and writes, it does not pop; the pop happens at the Guarded response handler (tmux.rs:172) via cmd_queue.pop_front().

Race / correctness gap: if push_command_capped runs while state == WaitingForResponse and queue.len() reaches 50_001, drain drops the in-flight head. When the Guarded response arrives, pop_front() pops a DIFFERENT pending command and calls cmd.process_result(domain_id, &response) on the wrong command — cross-wiring response payload onto the wrong command parser. Both threads hold the cmd_queue lock so the drain+pop ordering is deterministic under the lock, but the correctness violation stands.

Fix options (not applied here, pattern-level):
 1. When state == WaitingForResponse, skip index 0 in drain: queue.drain(1..=excess) (but preserves head).
 2. Drain from the back (newest-first) which discards most recently enqueued layout/session events that often re-fire.
 3. Abort the in-flight state machine (set state=Idle) and drain — heavy-handed but restores invariant.

Only triggers in pathological event storms (remote tmux lag + 50_000 queued commands) so severity is MEDIUM. The original bug fixed in 1055cffc (push-site cap enforcement) traded unbounded memory for this subtle correctness edge. A regression test would drive tmux into WaitingForResponse, push >50_001 Layout/SessionChanged events, then fire a Guarded response and assert process_result is NOT called against a different command type than the one that was sent.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T17:16:32.972876Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.760204Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T17:34:41.759836Z","close_reason":"Shipped in 9cc7e6c2. push_command_capped migrated from free function to TmuxDomainState method so it can consult self.state before choosing drain range:
- state == WaitingForResponse → drain(1..=excess) (preserves index 0, the in-flight cmd awaiting its Guarded response)
- any other state → drain(..excess) (retains existing drop-oldest semantics)

Both drain branches log distinctly so event-storm incidents are traceable. Lock ordering matches Guarded-handler's (cmd_queue first, state transiently inside method): no deadlock. send_next_command's cap-drain is untouched — already guarded by early 'state != Idle → return'.

Regression coverage: 2 new tests under mux::tmux::tests that each seat a distinguishable SplitPane at index 0 and push CMD_QUEUE_MAX_DEPTH more commands; one flips state to WaitingForResponse and asserts the head's get_command() text is unchanged (pre-fix fails), the other keeps state Idle and asserts the head IS drained (pins against future over-correction). Existing cmd_queue_hard_cap_drains_oldest_when_exceeded still green after the refactor.

Verification via rch exec -- env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-p5-orch-target:
- cargo check -p mux --lib: clean
- cargo test -p mux --lib cmd_queue: 3/3 green.

Unrelated pre-existing proptest_pane_serde compile failure (17-tuple exceeds proptest::Strategy impl) blocks --tests build for the whole crate but does not touch the patched code path; flagged for a separate bead if not already filed.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"ft-wk5fo","title":"move FTS5 indexing off the capture-write transaction","description":"# Background

Follow-up to ft-kcsae (answered). Live capture path in storage.rs
writes synchronously INTO the FTS5 index via three AFTER-triggers:

    storage.rs:169  output_segments_ai AFTER INSERT  → INSERT INTO output_segments_fts
    storage.rs:173  output_segments_ad AFTER DELETE  → fts delete
    storage.rs:177  output_segments_au AFTER UPDATE  → fts delete + insert

Every captured segment pays the inverted-index rebuild cost before
`append_segment_sync` returns (storage.rs:12308 → 12335 INSERT INTO
output_segments). Under 200-pane fanout the trigger cost is paid 200x
per-wire-tick.

# Existing primitives that can be reused

- `fts_pane_progress` table (storage.rs:472) — already tracks per-pane
  last-indexed-seq with resumable offsets.
- `sync_fts_on_startup(conn, FtsSyncConfig)` (storage.rs:16131) — the
  batched catchup engine. Currently only fires at startup.
- `FtsSyncConfig` (storage.rs:2363) — batch_size=100, max_batch_bytes=1
  MB, commit_progress per batch. Ready knobs.

# Proposal

1. Drop the three AFTER triggers on output_segments.
2. Add a writer-thread ticker (every N seconds OR after M segments
   since last catchup) that runs `sync_fts_on_startup` with the
   existing FtsSyncConfig and uses `fts_pane_progress` to pick up
   where it left off.
3. Gate behind a `storage.fts_indexing_mode = 'sync' | 'deferred'`
   config flag; default 'sync' at first, flip to 'deferred' after a
   round of search-freshness observability.
4. Expose catchup-lag (now - max(fts_pane_progress.last_indexed_at))
   as a metric so operators can spot index drift.

# Risk

- Search freshness becomes eventual (default lag = catchup interval).
- FTS rows can briefly diverge from output_segments during a crash
  between INSERT and the next catchup tick — acceptable because
  `sync_fts_on_startup` already handles gap recovery on startup.

# Measurement prerequisite

Before landing: add a bench that measures `append_segment_sync` p99
under a 200-pane load with FTS5 enabled (current) vs. triggers
disabled + deferred catchup. If the gap is not material, don't ship —
the synchronous path is simpler and the trigger tax might be in the
noise given SQLite's WAL + page cache.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"pane1","owner":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","created_at":"2026-04-21T18:00:59.588862Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:00.206770Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T11:28:00.206400Z","close_reason":"Opt-in deferred FTS indexing landed as d74401c6. Added StorageConfig.defer_fts_triggers: bool (default false, preserves current sync behavior). When true, StorageHandle::with_config executes DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS on output_segments_a[iud] immediately after initialize_schema, so new segment writes no longer synchronously rebuild FTS inverted-index pages. Callers are responsible for periodic sync_fts() invocation; the fts_pane_progress + sync_fts_on_startup engine already handles resumable batched catchup. Verified via 4 new regression tests in fts_sync_tests (17 total passing): fts_sync_mode_indexes_on_insert (baseline), fts_deferred_mode_does_not_index_on_insert (post-DROP empty FTS), fts_deferred_mode_catchup_indexes_all_segments (500 segments → catchup → 500 indexed, second catchup is no-op), fts_deferred_mode_catchup_resumes_across_rounds (5 rounds × 100 segments interleaved with catchup, each round indexes exactly 100). Surfaced but did NOT fix: pre-existing seq=0 off-by-one in sync_fts_for_pane (WHERE seq > last_indexed_seq excludes fresh pane's seq=0 segment) — tests use seq 1..=N, separate bead for that fix.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3602,"issue_id":"ft-wk5fo","author":"pane3-fts5-scaffold","text":"Claimed for scaffolding. Build verification currently blocked by ft-h9g0q (non-exhaustive match on new Error variants in error_renderer.rs:34 + retry.rs:378). Just shipped a mechanical fix in commit 87d4546b covering all 3 new typed variants; waiting on cold cargo check to confirm green. Once compile is green, scope for this bead's first shippable slice is the criterion benchmark for append_segment_sync p99 described in the ticket's 'Measurement prerequisite' — no implementation changes before we have numbers.","created_at":"2026-04-21T20:17:34Z"}]}
